data.acceptance:
1. When `--env` is set and the child command's basename is one of `sh`, `bash`, `zsh`, `fish`, `pwsh`, `cmd.exe`, `powershell`, abort with: "use stdin or `--allow-shell-env`; child shell will export the value."
2. Same for `docker run` / `podman run` without `--env-file=-` or `-e KEY` pointing only at this var.
3. `--allow-shell-env` flag exists for users who really mean it.
4. README "Safe Secret Entry" section gains a paragraph explaining when `--env` is and isn't safe.
